name: Enhance Chat Responses with Real-Time Search Data via Bright Data & Gemini AI
description: Automated workflow: Enhance Chat Responses with Real-Time Search Data via Bright Data & Gemini AI. This workflow integrates 11 different services: stickyNote, toolHttpRequest, lmChatGoogleGemini, agent, mcpClientTool. It contains 21 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.
shortDescription: Enhance Chat Responses with Real-Time Search Data via Bright Data & Gemini AI workflow containing 18 nodes.

nodes.name: Yandex Search Engine for Bright Data
nodes.notes: Scrape search results from Google, Bing or Yandex. Returns SERP results in markdown (URL, title, description)

nodes.name: Sticky Note3

parameters.content:
## Note
Use Bright Data MCP Search Engine assistant tools to perform Google, Bing or Yandex Search.

The AI Agent will make use of suitable search engine-based tools, returns the response to Chat and also performs the Webhook notification call for sending the AI responses via the MCP Client tools.

Source - {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note4

parameters.content:
## LLM Usage
Google Gemini is employed by the AI agent to understand and interpret user queries. Based on this interpretation, the agent initiates a call to the appropriate MCP client to perform the required web search task.

parameters.toolName: ={{ $('MCP Client list all tools for Bright Data').item.json.tools[0].name }}

nodes.name: MCP Client list all tools for Bright Data

parameters.content:
## Disclaimer
This template is only available on n8n self-hosted as it's making use of the community node for MCP Client.

## Note
Use Bright Data MCP Search Engine assistant tools to perform Google, Bing or Yandex Search.

The AI Agent will make use of suitable search engine-based tools, returns the response to Chat and also performs the Webhook notification call for sending the AI responses via the MCP Client tools.

Source - {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

## LLM Usage
Google Gemini is employed by the AI agent to understand and interpret user queries. Based on this interpretation, the agent initiates a call to the appropriate MCP client to perform the required web search task.

nodes.name: Yandex Search Engine for Bright Data

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: google Palm  A P I details
mcp Client  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "When clicking ‘Test workflow’".
Open the "When chat message received" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "AI Agent" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Google Gemini Chat Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Simple Memory" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "MCP Client list all tools for Bright Data" node (n8n-nodes-mcp.mcpClient) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "MCP Client Bright Data Search Tool" node (n8n-nodes-mcp.mcpClient) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Google Search Engine for Bright Data" node (n8n-nodes-mcp.mcpClientTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Bing Search Engine for Bright Data" node (n8n-nodes-mcp.mcpClientTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "MCP Client List all tools" node (n8n-nodes-mcp.mcpClientTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "HTTP Request for Webhook Notification" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Yandex Search Engine for Bright Data" node (n8n-nodes-mcp.mcpClientTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Set search query".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
